description_long: This object is a vintage-style television with a boxy, rectangular wooden cabinet and a prominent, slightly convex glass screen. The front features a large display area with static lines, flanked on the right by a vertical panel containing several round control buttons and a speaker grille. The lower section of the front has two rectangular recesses, possibly for additional controls or decorative panels. The back of the television is solid with ventilation slots and a small panel for input/output ports, while the sides are plain and finished in a wood grain texture. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century analog televisions, with a sturdy, utilitarian build and minimal modern embellishments.
description: A vintage box-style television with a wood grain cabinet, convex glass screen, and side control panel. The back features ventilation slots and a port panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass screen, control panel with buttons and speaker grille, and two lower rectangular recesses.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain, wood-textured surface with no visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing ventilation slots and a small input/output port panel near the bottom right.
description_view_3: Right side view with a plain, wood-textured surface similar to the left side.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, brown wood bezel with a matte finish and rectangular styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of round buttons on the front right panel
